# Button

Web component `nds-button`. Aligned with the Figma Design System 2026: variants (intent) primary, secondary, ghost, accent, info, positive, negative, warning; sizes xs/sm/md/lg; radius standard/full; leading/trailing slots for icons.

## Preview and playground

Live preview of the web component with controls for variant, size, and radius (aligned with Figma). The Light/Dark theme of the web app applies automatically.
<Playground />

## Button size guidelines

Button size should adapt to device context and interaction needs, ensuring adequate tap targets on mobile and a clear visual hierarchy for high-priority actions.

| Device  | Default | High-priority actions | Tables |
|--------|--------|----------------------|--------|
| Desktop | `SM` | `LG` | `XS` |
| Mobile  | `MD` | `XL` | `MD` |


## Accessibility
This component is designed to support **WCAG 2.2 Level AA**. Color contrast and size requirements have already been verified by the design team. The guidance below provides implementation recommendations to help ensure accessible behavior in code.
### Focus state preview

### Implementation recommendations
- **Disabled state is not allowed**. Buttons must not use the disabled state to prevent interaction. Users must always be able to activate the control. If the action cannot be completed, the interface must clearly show the relevant errors and explain why the action cannot be performed and what needs to be corrected.
- **Keyboard access**. Buttons must be reachable using the `Tab` key and can be activated with `Space` or `Enter`.
- **Icon-only buttons**. When a button does not have a visible label (for example icon-only buttons), it must include an accessible label. The label should be exposed to assistive technologies and appear on hover or focus.
- **Links styled as buttons**. If a link has been repurposed to behave like a button, it must also respond to the `Space` key. By default, links are activated only with `Enter`, so additional scripting is required to support `Space`.

See the [WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices – Button Pattern](https://www.w3.org/TR/wai-aria-practices-1.2/#button) for further implementation considerations.

### Accessibility testing results
| Component | Test | Status |
|:----------|:--------|:--------|
| Button | Default state | <AccessibilityTag variant="tested" /> |
| | Advanced states | <AccessibilityTag variant="tested" /> |
| | Keyboard navigation | <AccessibilityTag variant="dev" /> |
| | Screen reader | <AccessibilityTag variant="dev" /> |



## Usage

Import `@namirial/components/loader`, call `defineCustomElements()`, then use the `<nds-button variant="primary">` tag.

```html
<nds-button variant="primary" size="md">Label</nds-button>
